title: crataegus oxyacantha subsp. maura
intro: Crataegus oxyacantha subsp. maura is a subspecies of the hawthorn tree, native to Europe and Western Asia, characterized by its dense, thorny branches and showy white flowers that give way to red or purple berries. The medicinal properties of Crataegus oxyacantha subsp. maura are attributed to its flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds, which have been traditionally used to support heart health, lower blood pressure, and improve cardiovascular function. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving circulation, and preventing oxidative stress, making it a popular herbal remedy for cardiovascular disorders. Crataegus oxyacantha subsp. maura can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties and provide a range of health benefits.
